title: Antibiotics
puzzle: Why did antibiotics transform medicine so dramatically?
insight: Antibiotics target bacteria in ways that help the body overcome infections that were once often deadly, but their power depends on careful use.
key_ideas: They work against bacteria, not viruses.
Their discovery changed surgery, infection care, and survival.
mind_blower: A medicine that once felt miraculous can slowly lose power if humans teach microbes how to resist it.
next_curiosity: If antibiotics can stop working, how should medicine respond to resistance?
narration_text: Why did antibiotics transform medicine so dramatically? Antibiotics target bacteria in ways that help the body overcome infections that were once often deadly, but their power depends on careful use. They work against bacteria, not viruses. Overuse can encourage resistance. Their discovery changed surgery, infection care, and survival. A medicine that once felt miraculous can slowly lose power if humans teach microbes how to resist it. If antibiotics can stop working, how should medicine respond to resistance?
